title: Pisos epoxi para aulas, cafeterías y pasillos - Elite Mexico
content:
Classrooms, Cafeterias & Hallways
The two most commonly used flooring products specified for educational facilities are cost-effective vinyl (VCT) or expensive terrazzo. Our seamless resinous flooring systems compete with the lower cost associated with VCT while offering the high-end durability of terrazzo. These surfaces meet or exceed ADA Flat Surfaces, ADA Inclined Surfaces while also USDA, FDA and CFIA acceptable.
Advantages
Easy to clean and maintain
Ultra-durable, high abrasion resistance withstands heavy traffic
Fluid applied systems for new construction or existing floor restoration
Finish options include high gloss or satin
Variable coefficient of friction (COF) levels to meet or exceed ADA Flat Surfaces, ADA Inclined Surfaces
USDA, FDA* and CFIA** acceptable.
Antimicrobial and stain resistant
Seamless flooring, no grout lines to harbor bacteria
* Can be used in USDA facilities based on FSIS Directive 11,000.4 (Rev. 1), November 24, 1995.
** Agriculture Canada (also known as Canadian Food Inspection Agency).
